summary: Tested mobile UX overhaul (viewport 390x844) and verified desktop did not regress (1920x800). Found ONE CRITICAL layout bug: MobileSubBar (search pill + 4 tabs) renders at y=5706px (end of feed) instead of sticky below topbar. Root cause: .archive-layout CSS grid only declares 'topbar/main' rows; <MobileSubBar/> has no grid-area so it lands in implicit row after main-area. Visible side-effect: the 4 mobile tabs are completely off the visible area, and force-click navigates to the wrong destination because the bottom SideRail intercepts the click after scrollIntoView. All other mobile requirements pass: sticky topbar, opaque drawer with working search ('kei' returned users, 'roma' returned 5 relatos), 5-item bottom nav with no gap, symmetric card centering (x=12, right_margin=12), composer icons + PUBLICAR visible, hashtag pills present, PTR indicator triggers, bottom-sheet modal works. Desktop preserves SearchBar inline, 4 nav tabs and full side rail (Inicio/Explorar/Crónicas/Biblioteca/Mi legado/Épocas/Efemérides/Legados/Misivas + Salir + avatar). Backend endpoints /api/buscar, /api/misivas, /api/relatos all return 200.

minor.issue: Path does not exist in server.js (404). Review request mentioned it but actual mi-legado endpoints use different paths. Confirm intended URL or update doc.
404 — correct path is /api/misivas (returns array of conversations).

ui_bugs.issue: MobileSubBar is rendered inside .archive-layout (CSS grid) but the grid only declares rows 'topbar' and 'main'. The subbar has no grid-area, so it gets auto-placed in an implicit row AFTER .main-area. Measured bounding-box: y=5706px (end of feed), height=64px. Although CSS marks it position:sticky;top:56px;z-index:80, sticky has no effect because the subbar's containing block is positioned at the bottom of the document — user never sees the search pill nor the 4 tabs (Para ti / Crónicas / Legados / Documentos) below the topbar. Mockups required these to be the second visible row of the mobile UI.

ui_bugs.component: MobileSubBar tabs navigation (consequence of layout bug)
ui_bugs.issue: Because the subbar sits at y≈5706 (off-screen), Playwright force-clicks scroll the page first, then the fixed bottom .side-rail (z=100) intercepts the click. Observed: click 'Crónicas' → URL becomes /explorar; click 'Legados' → /documentos; click 'Documentos' → /mi-legado (each tab navigates to the rail item it visually overlaps after scroll). Will resolve automatically once the layout bug above is fixed (subbar at viewport top).

mobile_checks_passed: Sticky topbar (y=0 before and after window.scrollTo(0,800))
Drawer visually opaque (linear-gradient bg, screenshot confirmed)
Drawer inline search returns real results: 'kei' → 1 user (Keilin), 'roma' → 5 relatos
Bottom nav 5 items (Inicio, Explorar, Crónicas, Biblioteca, Mi legado) fixed at y+h=844 (no gap)
Feed cards centered: .social-post / .weekly-highlight / .feed-greeting / .social-composer all at x=12, width=366, right margin=12 (symmetric)
Hashtag pills rendered inside .social-post-content (10 pills found across feed)
Composer shows Imagen/Época/Relato icons and PUBLICAR button visible
Pull-to-refresh: indicator element appears (count=1) after touch drag from top
desktop_checks_passed: Desktop nav tabs visible (nav-para-ti, nav-crónicas, nav-legados, nav-documentos)
SideRail shows 10 items including epocas, efemerides, legados, misivas + rail-logout + rail-user-avatar
action_items: Fix .archive-layout grid to allocate a row for the mobile subbar. Suggested: in @media (max-width: 900px) change grid-template-rows to '56px auto 1fr' and grid-template-areas to '"topbar" "subbar" "main"', and add .mobile-subbar { grid-area: subbar; } at mobile breakpoint. Alternatively, move <MobileSubBar/> out of .archive-layout (render it as direct sibling of the topbar with position:sticky on body), or assign it via grid-row: 2 explicitly.
After layout fix, re-verify tab navigation: clicking each mobile-tab should land on its own path (/, /cronicas, /legados, /documentos).
Optional: confirm whether /api/mi-legado/perfil and /api/misivas/conversaciones should exist or if review request had stale paths.
critical_code_review_comments: PageShell.js: <MobileSubBar/> is rendered as a direct child of .archive-layout (a CSS grid), but no grid-area is assigned anywhere in mobile-app.css for .mobile-subbar — this is the root cause of the misplacement.
MobileSubBar uses sticky positioning relying on the parent being a scroll container; verify the new grid row also acts correctly with the surrounding overflow:clip applied to html/body.
Side-rail z-index (100) > mobile-subbar z-index (80); once subbar is at top this is fine, but worth keeping in mind for any future overlap scenarios.

success_rate.frontend: 12/13 mobile checks passed (~92%) — only subbar placement failing; 100% desktop

context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the grid layout so MobileSubBar appears between topbar and main, re-test on 390x844: (1) measure bounding box of [data-testid="mobile-subbar"] expecting y≈56, (2) click each mobile-tab-{para-ti,crónicas,legados,documentos} and assert URL changes to /, /cronicas, /legados, /documentos respectively, (3) sticky check: scroll to 800 and confirm subbar y stays ≈56. All other mobile UX requirements already pass.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: CSS grid layout .archive-layout declares only two grid areas (topbar/main on mobile). The MobileSubBar component (added in PageShell.js between TopbarArchive and SideRail in DOM order) lacks any grid-area: subbar assignment. CSS Grid therefore auto-places the unassigned item into an implicit row generated AFTER the explicit template, placing the subbar at the end of the document (y=5706 in the tested feed). The element's position:sticky;top:56px has no visible effect because, by the time it would stick, the viewport has already scrolled past its containing-block. As a consequence, the 4 mobile navigation tabs are never visible to the user and force-click navigation lands on the SideRail item that happens to overlap the same coordinates after Playwright's auto-scroll. Fix: extend grid template to include a subbar row OR remove the subbar from the grid context and render it as a fixed/sticky overlay sibling of topbar.
